glossary.definition: A mathematical structure formed by a collection of elements called vectors, which may be added together and multiplied by numbers (scalars), satisfying closure, associativity, commutativity, and distributivity axioms.
A basis for a vector space where all basis vectors are mutually orthogonal (perpendicular) and have unit length (norm of 1).
The square root of a non-zero eigenvalue of the matrix product A^T * A or A * A^T, representing the scaling factor of a matrix transformation along its principal axes.

references.title: The Elements of Statistical Learning
references.author: Trevor Hastie, Robert Tibshirani, Jerome Friedman

further_reading.title: Linear Algebra and Learning from Data
further_reading.author: Gilbert Strang

research_papers.title: Matrix Factorization Techniques for Recommender Systems
research_papers.authors: Yehuda Koren, Robert Bell, Chris Volinsky
